showcaseMilestones.title: Set the event framework
showcaseMilestones.action: Select the public format, tentative date and time, intended audience, venue, and administrative or district support.

showcaseMilestones.title: Confirm the audience and space
showcaseMilestones.action: Reserve the venue, confirm the public audience pathway, send a save-the-date, and begin district communication or visitor approval steps.

showcaseMilestones.title: Lock invitations and logistics
showcaseMilestones.action: Send formal invitations and lock technology, accessibility, room flow, assessment workflow, logistics owners, and backup plans.

showcaseMilestones.title: Refine the message and confirm the run of show
showcaseMilestones.action: Students refine their research story and practice; the teacher confirms the event schedule, guest roles, technology plan, accessibility supports, and presentation order.

showcaseMilestones.title: Prepare for questions
showcaseMilestones.action: Students rehearse evidence-based Q&A while the teacher confirms guest guidance and feedback collection.

showcaseMilestones.title: Complete the final readiness check
showcaseMilestones.action: Verify student readiness, backup plans, room setup, visitor flow, technology, accessibility, event-day printing, and signage.

showcaseMilestones.title: Host the required public showcase
showcaseMilestones.action: Every student presents to a genuine public audience, answers questions, collects feedback, and demonstrates professionalism.

showcaseMilestones.title: Reflect, acknowledge, and archive
showcaseMilestones.action: Students complete reflection and portfolio closeout; the teacher archives artifacts and sends acknowledgments or thank-you communication.

weeks.focus: Course launch, research culture, curiosity, and ethics
weeks.note: Launch research culture, student identity, interests, and ethics norms. Optional supports include the project card sort, initial family communication, and differentiation notes.

weeks.focus: Experimental design, feasibility, and preliminary research questions
weeks.note: Move from broad interests toward feasible, testable research questions; use the Unit 1 teacher checklist as an end-of-unit reference.

weeks.focus: From project idea to testable plan
weeks.note: Refine project ideas into an evidence-based, testable plan; revisit Deck 4 language for variables, controls, criteria, and constraints. Differentiation notes are optional teacher reference.

weeks.focus: Formal research plan, safety, ethics, and approval
weeks.note: Use Deck 3 as an ethics and safety revisit. The Project Approval Tracker is the official approval record; the Unit 2 teacher checklist and district-supplied forms are teacher references.

weeks.focus: Launching pilot testing and research journals
weeks.note: Begin only after the tracker records approval for pilot testing; use Unit 3 Appendix V as the teacher scoring reference for research journals.
Use pilot evidence to troubleshoot and document method decisions.

weeks.focus: Revising methods and data collection tools
weeks.note: Turn pilot learning into a revised procedure and stronger data tools.
Systematic collection begins only after the tracker records approval for systematic data collection.

weeks.focus: Continuing data collection and monitoring quality
weeks.note: Continue collection while checking data quality.
Use conferences and mentor feedback without transferring ownership away from students.
Focus on repeated trials, reliability, and completeness.
Complete the midpoint progress report and teacher conference; use Unit 3 Appendix V when scoring the journal checkpoint.
Turn midpoint feedback into a documented action plan; revisit Deck 8 when recovery requires a responsible revision or pivot.

weeks.focus: Completing major trials or prototype iterations
weeks.note: Check whether major trials or prototype iterations are complete.
Organize and audit data before analysis.

weeks.focus: Unit 3 closure and readiness for analysis
weeks.note: Close Unit 3, complete the final Unit 3 journal check with Appendix V, and verify analysis readiness.
Build a clean, auditable data set before analysis.
Match data and questions to suitable analysis methods.

weeks.focus: Graphs, tables, visuals, error, and uncertainty
weeks.note: Create honest visuals and account for error and uncertainty.

weeks.focus: Interpretation, claims, and transition to reporting
weeks.note: Move from analysis into defensible claims and reporting readiness.

Teacher-only showcase milestone 1: select the public format, tentative Week 33 date and time, audience, venue, and administrative or district support. No added student deliverable or print requirement.
Draft the report introduction.
Draft a reproducible methods section.
Teacher-only showcase milestone 2: reserve space, confirm the public audience pathway, send a save-the-date, and begin district communication. No added student deliverable or print requirement.
Write the discussion using claims, evidence, reasoning, and limitations.
Assemble the complete research report.

Use Unit 5 Appendix T when the local plan scores peer-review quality. Teacher-only showcase milestone 3: send formal invitations and lock technology, accessibility, room flow, assessment workflow, logistics owners, and backup plans. No added student showcase deliverable or print requirement.
Design the presentation product and begin formal practice. Use Unit 6 Appendix L as the optional teacher scoring reference for final oral criteria.

weeks.focus: Final presentation readiness and message refinement
weeks.note: Refine the research story and speaking plan; use the final oral rubric as an optional practice reference.
Practice for the actual public audience; use the final oral rubric as an optional feedback reference.

weeks.focus: Q&A preparation and defense of evidence
weeks.note: Prepare to answer questions with evidence and scientific humility; use the final oral rubric as an optional scoring reference.
Finalize student readiness and teacher event operations; use the final oral rubric as an optional rehearsal reference.
Every student presents to a genuine public audience. Unit 6 Appendix L is the final oral rubric; Appendix M scores the showcase presentation.

weeks.focus: Final reflection, portfolio, and next steps
weeks.note: Close the course with reflection, portfolio, feedback, and optional next steps.
